UK Biker Hazard Guide

Seasonal hazards every rider should know
🐑

Sheep

Year-round (peaks Mar-Oct)
Risk Level
85%

Common on moorland B-roads in Wales, Scotland, and northern England. They bolt without warning — always ride at a speed where you can stop within the road you can see.

🦌

Deer

Oct-Nov (rutting) & May-Jun (fawns)
Risk Level
75%

Dawn and dusk are highest risk. Watch for deer-crossing signs on A-roads through forested areas. Highlands and New Forest are hotspots.

🧊

Ice & Frost

Nov-Mar
Risk Level
95%

Shaded bends stay icy when the rest of the road is clear. Mountain passes freeze overnight. Check road surface temperature — if below 3°C, assume ice is present.

⚠️

Gravel & Mud

Year-round (worst Oct-Apr)
Risk Level
70%

Farm entrances, hedge cutting, and flood aftermath deposit debris on bends. Rural B-roads after rain are worst. Scan the road surface constantly.

🛢️

Diesel Spills

Year-round
Risk Level
80%

Roundabouts, fuel station approaches, and industrial estate exits are highest risk. Rainbow sheen on wet road means diesel — treat like ice. Urban and suburban roads near lorry routes are worst.
